arts.ttl: Chelsea fans send message to Manchester United after Jadon Sancho’s display against Heidenheim
arts.sum: Chelsea secured a fourth consecutive victory in the Europa Conference League as they ran out 2-0 winners over Heidenheim on Thursday night. The Blues triumphed...

arts.ttl: What Bruno Fernandes did after Alejandro Garnacho's early Man United goal speaks volumes
arts.sum: Manchester United got off to the perfect start against Bodo/Glimt in the Europa League during Ruben Amorim's Old Trafford debut

arts.ttl: Manchester United paid £21.4m to replace Ten Hag with Amorim
arts.sum: Sacking Dutchman and staff cost £10.4m United spent £11m to hire new coaching team Manchester United have revealed it cost £21.4m to sack Erik ten Hag as their manager and hire Ruben Amorim as the Dutchman’s replacement. Ten Hag was removed on 28 October, with his coaching staff René Hake, Jelle ten Rouwelaar, Pieter Morel and Ruud van Nistelrooy all following. A total £10.4m was paid in compensation to do so. Signing Amorim, plus the coaching quintet of Carlos Fernandes, Jorge Vital, Adélio Cândido, Emanuel Ferro and Paulo Barreira who followed him from Sporting, was priced at £11m, the club’s latest accounts state. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Man United are gifted a goal just FIFTY seconds into Ruben Amorim's Old Trafford debut - after comical gaffe from Bodo/Glimt goalkeeper
arts.sum: Manchester United capitalised on a comical goalkeeping error to score just 50 seconds into Ruben Amorim's Old Trafford debut on Thursday night.

arts.ttl: Man Utd line up four-man shortlist to solve Amorim striker woes
arts.sum: Kolo Muani could be the most likely option due to PSG’s desire to get rid of their €95m man after just 18 months at the club, however the 25-year-old has ‘not been a hot topic’ at United despite his availability on loan or permanently. Amorim has also insisted that he has faith in his attacking talent to provide goals and that he ‘doesn’t need new strikers to score’ but United are still considering all options behind the scenes.

arts.ttl: 'It is important' - Ruben Amorim explains decision to give Manchester United player shock chance
arts.sum: Man Utd take on Bodo/Glimt in the Europa League tonight and Ruben Amorim spoke ahead of kick-off.

arts.ttl: Rio Ferdinand agrees with Ruben Amorim verdict on Viktor Gyokeres to Man United transfer
arts.sum: Rio Ferdinand and Martin Keown have had their say on Viktor Gyokeres amid transfer links with Manchester United and Arsenal

arts.ttl: Manchester United v Bodø/Glimt: Europa League – live
arts.sum: Europa League updates from the 8pm GMT kick-off Live scoreboard | Tottenham v Roma | And mail Will Kevin Wilson emails: “If Amorim can get Mount anywhere near his Chelsea prime, then that will be a huge bonus. He’s still young and despite a rough few years, he’s still very talented. Whether he works better as one of the deeper midfielders or in the front three remains to be seen, but if he can stay fit, he’ll give the manager options.” Amorim: “I am really calm, I expect a good game, a good environment, I am feeling that belonging with the fans. I am expecting the team to show different things, we need to improve.” Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Team News: Man United XI for Bodo/Glimt revealed as Ruben Amorim names first Old Trafford lineup
arts.sum: Manchester United head coach Ruben Amorim names his starting XI for Thursday's Europa League contest with Bodo/Glimt at Old Trafford.

arts.ttl: Manchester turns YELLOW as a sea of Bodo/Glimt fans arrive in force to cheer on the Norwegian side against Man United in Ruben Amorim's Old Trafford debut
arts.sum: Fans of the Norwegian outfit, who currently sit above the Red Devils in the Europa League table ahead of Thursday's clash at Old Trafford, were in high spirits before kick-off.
